channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-12: 5 commits across 2 repos
item.description:
Daily digest — 2026-06-12

2 repos active, 5 total commits.

  • metacheck: 1 commit on 2026-06-12
  • docpluck: 4 commits on 2026-06-12

View full dashboard

item.title: metacheck — 1 commit on 2026-06-12
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck — 2026-06-12

In the 'scienceverse/metacheck' project, work was done to integrate a TUE (Technische Universiteit Eindhoven) grobid server. Specifically, a commit by Lisa DeBruine added this server, which is likely used for parsing and extracting metadata from scientific documents. This addition aims to enhance the project's capabilities in processing and analyzing scientific literature.

1 commit on 2026-06-12:

  • [feat] d85d73f Add TUE grobid server — Lisa DeBruine

item.title: docpluck — 4 commits on 2026-06-12
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck — 2026-06-12

In the 'giladfeldman/docpluck' project, work was done on fixing issues with reference splitting, page breaks, and category labels. Specifically, commits addressed problems with Harvard reference splitting, quantifier-head guards, and deterministic diffs. Additionally, updates were made to the documentation and continuous integration (CI) process, including installing poppler and adding new lessons.

4 commits on 2026-06-12:

  • [fix] 8bfcdba fix(v2.4.85): Harvard ref-splitting (D1) + page-break stitch & category-label header strip (D2) — Gilad Feldman
  • [fix] b552181 ci: apt-get update before poppler install (fix stale-list 404 flake) — Gilad Feldman
  • [other] 2804ac1 docs(learnings): v2.4.84 R2 quantifier-head — verify-the-layer lesson + deterministic-diff-as-canary — Gilad Feldman
  • [fix] 28c4910 fix(v2.4.84): R2 quantifier-head guard — stop deleting digits from reference titles — Gilad Feldman

item.title: metacheck — 1 commit on 2026-06-11
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck — 2026-06-11

In the 'scienceverse/metacheck' project, work was done to add an email feature. Specifically, a commit by Lakens on 2026-06-11 added email functionality. This addition suggests that the project is being developed to include notification or communication capabilities via email.

1 commit on 2026-06-11:

  • [feat] fe37300 add email — Lakens

item.title: coarse — 1 commit on 2026-06-11
item.description:
Davidvandijcke/coarse — 2026-06-11

In the 'Davidvandijcke/coarse' project, work was done to resolve an issue with subscription handoffs. Specifically, a fix was implemented to stop requiring an OpenRouter key for non-PDF subscription handoffs. This change was made in response to issues #186 and #217.

1 commit on 2026-06-11:

  • [fix] 3af872e fix(web): stop demanding an OpenRouter key for non-PDF subscription handoffs (#186) (#217) — David Van Dijcke

item.title: metacheck — 3 commits on 2026-06-10
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck — 2026-06-10

In the 'scienceverse/metacheck' project, work was done on updating the Grobid import and fixing the bibliography title. These changes were merged from the 'dev' branch, suggesting that they were part of a development cycle. The updates aimed to improve the import functionality and correct formatting issues with bibliography titles.

3 commits on 2026-06-10:

  • [chore] 875a2af Merge pull request #306 from scienceverse/dev — Lisa DeBruine
  • [fix] 865885a bib title fix — Lisa DeBruine
  • [other] 4ae6924 grobid import updates — Lisa DeBruine

item.title: metacheck — 1 commit on 2026-06-09
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck — 2026-06-09

In the 'scienceverse/metacheck' project, work was done on improving imports, as evidenced by Lisa DeBruine's commit on June 9, 2026. This commit, titled "import improvements", suggests that the focus was on refining and enhancing the import functionality within the project. The specific details of these improvements are not provided, but it indicates an effort to optimize the import process.

1 commit on 2026-06-09:

  • [other] a9ed9f4 import improvements — Lisa DeBruine

item.title: docpluck — 2 commits on 2026-06-09
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck — 2026-06-09

In the 'giladfeldman/docpluck' project, work was done on the deployment of docpluck, specifically fixing the `/health` version-match check to be the authoritative gate and updating the `/extract` smoke test to be authentication-aware. Additionally, documentation updates were made, including notes on follow-ups for the v2.4.83 release and a ScienceArena arena-report verdict. These changes aim to improve the reliability and authentication of the docpluck deployment.

2 commits on 2026-06-09:

  • [fix] 849cd53 fix(docpluck-deploy): /health version-match is authoritative gate; /extract smoke now auth-aware (was stale, false-failed on auth-gated endpoint) — Gilad Feldman
  • [chore] 905c02b docs(todo): v2.4.83 release follow-ups + ScienceArena arena-report verdict — Gilad Feldman

item.title: coarse — 7 commits on 2026-06-09
item.description:
Davidvandijcke/coarse — 2026-06-09

In the 'Davidvandijcke/coarse' project, work was done on releasing versions 1.6.0 and 1.7.0, which included adding Claude Fable 5 to the model picker, recalibrating the cost estimate for reasoning models, and implementing per-comment Discuss chat streaming directly to OpenRouter. Additionally, the default model was set to Qwen 3.7 Plus. These changes were made in preparation for the new releases.

7 commits on 2026-06-09:

  • [chore] f2fdbc3 Merge pull request #216 from Davidvandijcke/release/v1.7.0 — David Van Dijcke
  • [chore] 3a4434e chore(release): v1.7.0 — Davidvandijcke
  • [feat] 2a7d4d0 feat(models): add Claude Fable 5 to the picker + gate its missing temperature (#214) (#215) — David Van Dijcke
  • [fix] 27782b9 fix(cost): recalibrate reasoning-model cost estimate; default Qwen 3.7 Plus (#212) (#213) — David Van Dijcke
  • [chore] 6da1c30 Merge pull request #211 from Davidvandijcke/release/v1.6.0 — David Van Dijcke
  • [chore] c0b2e61 chore(release): v1.6.0 — Davidvandijcke
  • [feat] 0756926 feat(web): per-comment Discuss chat streaming directly to OpenRouter (#209) (#210) — David Van Dijcke

item.title: docpluck — 7 commits on 2026-06-08
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck — 2026-06-08

In the 'giladfeldman/docpluck' project, work was done on improving the API, specifically adding a first-class `footnote_texts` API, and enhancing export and rendering capabilities, such as extracting PDF layouts and de-duplicating table labels. Additionally, several fixes were made to address issues with word integrity, running headers, and metadata leaks. These fixes were made across various versions, including v2.4.80, v2.4.81, v2.4.82, and v2.4.83.

7 commits on 2026-06-08:

  • [feat] fb0d595 feat(v2.4.83): first-class footnote_texts API + extract_pdf_layout export + render table-label de-dup — Gilad Feldman
  • [other] eed7708 docs(handoff): v2.4.82/v2.4.83 splice word-integrity + running-header strip — Gilad Feldman
  • [fix] c7555fc fix(v2.4.83): P0r strip bare "<Initials> <Surname> et al." running header — Gilad Feldman
  • [fix] 57c691c fix(v2.4.82): column-splice word-integrity (pre-existing corruption) + RC-1 Step1 general de-interleave (flag, dark) — Gilad Feldman
  • [fix] dacc675 fix(v2.4.81): RC-2 metadata/running-header leak strips (untested-corpus sweep) — Gilad Feldman
  • [fix] bd9aa56 docs(todo): deferred items from v2.4.80 ship (canary key bug, prod smoke, CitationGuard + ip_feldman follow-ups) — Gilad Feldman
  • [fix] a877982 fix(v2.4.80): O5 reference reading-order inversion (chen + jamison) — Gilad Feldman

item.title: docpluck — 8 commits on 2026-06-07
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck — 2026-06-07

In the 'giladfeldman/docpluck' project, work was done on resolving defects and issues, including clearing canary findings such as metadata leaks and hallucination false-positives, and addressing text-extraction defects. Additionally, efforts were made to improve testing efficiency with the addition of pytest-xdist for parallel testing. The project also involved creating and validating a region-aware column track prototype and documenting learnings and project lessons.

8 commits on 2026-06-07:

  • [other] 4771006 docs: region-aware column track — validated prototype + proven "no whole-page shortcut" constraint — Gilad Feldman
  • [other] 6ae9147 docs(cycle 5): diagnose ip_feldman B4+R4 as one column-interleave root cause (multi-session) — Gilad Feldman
  • [other] db4160b docs(cycle 5): LEARNINGS + project lessons + v2.4.79 handoff (findings #1/#2 cleared + canary-gate substrate) — Gilad Feldman
  • [fix] 76a0248 fix(v2.4.79): clear canary findings #1 (METADATA-LEAK) + #2 (HALLUCINATION false-positive) on ip_feldman + canary-gate substrate fixes — Gilad Feldman
  • [chore] b1d35a1 docs(handoff): text-extraction defects from citationguard-iterate (2026-06-07) — Gilad Feldman
  • [other] 6c67dc5 docs(handoff): 2026-06-07 v2.4.78 landed + canary-iterate next-session opener — Gilad Feldman
  • [feat] bcd1e04 chore: add pytest-xdist dev-dep (parallel suite ~19min) + todo.md v2.4.78 punch-list — Gilad Feldman
  • [fix] 73462e3 fix(v2.4.78): clear canary findings #1/#3/#4 + run-11 hallucinated-heading guards + soft-hyphen dehyphenation — Gilad Feldman

item.title: metacheck — 8 commits on 2026-06-05
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck — 2026-06-05

In the 'scienceverse/metacheck' repository, work was done on improving test coverage and reliability, including updating the test-coverage.yaml file and skipping a failing test on CRAN. Additionally, mocking was added for a test that fails on Continuous Integration (CI) and the httptest was removed. A static.yml file was also created as part of these changes.

8 commits on 2026-06-05:

  • [test] c5226cb Update test-coverage.yaml — Lisa DeBruine
  • [chore] def836e Merge pull request #298 from scienceverse/dev — Lisa DeBruine
  • [test] 33077a1 skip failing test on cran — Lisa DeBruine
  • [chore] 4bb7103 Merge pull request #297 from scienceverse/dev — Lisa DeBruine
  • [feat] fbed67b add mocking of test that fails on CI — Lisa DeBruine
  • [other] ff8328b Create static.yml — Lisa DeBruine
  • [chore] a1d7e77 Merge pull request #296 from scienceverse/dev — Lisa DeBruine
  • [other] c34339d removed httptest — Lisa DeBruine

item.title: metacheck — 10 commits on 2026-06-04
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck — 2026-06-04

In the 'scienceverse/metacheck' project, work was done on preparing a beta version release, including updating the README.md file and transitioning from an alpha to a beta version. Additionally, fixes were made to the prereg_check function and the package documentation was updated for a pkgdown release. The project also stopped auto tests as part of this update.

10 commits on 2026-06-04:

  • [chore] 09eb2d6 Merge pull request #295 from scienceverse/dev — Lisa DeBruine
  • [other] 6b96621 prereg_check fixes — Lisa DeBruine
  • [chore] 194f336 Merge pull request #294 from scienceverse/dev — Lisa DeBruine
  • [chore] c8aafa6 pkgdown release version — Lisa DeBruine
  • [chore] 7fbd052 Merge pull request #293 from scienceverse/dev — Lisa DeBruine
  • [other] 51514b6 update alpha->beta and stop auto tests — Lisa DeBruine
  • [docs] d8d1cdf Update README.md — Lisa DeBruine
  • [docs] df99f3c Update README.md — Lisa DeBruine
  • [chore] b047902 Merge pull request #292 from scienceverse/dev — Lisa DeBruine
  • [chore] f44fec7 beta version! — Lisa DeBruine
